search_queries.name: Claire
search_queries.priority_reason: Strongest warm pipeline lead. Booked a call 2026-04-07, confirmed booking-gap pain. LinkedIn is last channel. Extra caution required.
search_queries.linkedin_sector_priority: high — salons active on LinkedIn and social. Post_reference upgrade expected.
search_queries.fallback_query_1: site:linkedin.com/in hairdresser salon owner director "Leeds" "West Yorkshire"
search_queries.fallback_query_2: site:linkedin.com/in "Westfield Hair & Beauty" owner manager Leeds
search_queries.profile_verification_required: Confirm profile is Claire, owner/manager of Westfield Hair & Beauty in Leeds. Multiple staff may have profiles — verify role before connecting.
search_queries.do_not_send_if: IMAP reveals any email reply since 2026-04-04 — including any follow-up to the call booked 2026-04-07. Any reply cancels LinkedIn permanently.
search_queries.note_type_on_find: post_reference STRONGLY PREFERRED. Check posts from last 60 days FIRST. Salons post: bank holiday appointments, wedding hair, prom, spring colours, team news. Write bespoke note if post found. If no post: use generic note from linkedin_connection_notes.json.
search_queries.seasonal_context: May is peak salon season — wedding hair, prom, bank holiday appointments. Claire may have been too busy to progress in April. Pain point (no online booking) is structural and ongoing.

search_queries.name: Rob
search_queries.priority_reason: Highest revenue potential (£550-675 upsell). Upsell signal was the strongest in the campaign. IMAP especially critical — late reply is plausible.
search_queries.linkedin_sector_priority: high — B2B print/design well-represented on LinkedIn for client and supplier relationships.
search_queries.fallback_query_1: site:linkedin.com/in print design owner director "Sowerby Bridge" OR "Halifax" "West Yorkshire"
search_queries.fallback_query_2: site:linkedin.com/in "Pennine Print & Design" print owner manager director
search_queries.profile_verification_required: Confirm profile is Rob, owner/director of Pennine Print & Design in Sowerby Bridge. Do not connect to another Rob in print.
search_queries.do_not_send_if: IMAP reveals any email reply since 2026-04-04. Rob's upsell signal makes a late reply plausible — IMAP is especially critical before acting.
search_queries.note_type_on_find: post_reference if post found within last 60 days — B2B business posts likely (client work, events, print news, weddings, graduations). Write bespoke note. If no recent post: use generic note.
search_queries.seasonal_context: May is peak print season — weddings, events, graduations. Rob may have been too busy in April. Redesign pain is still live.

search_queries.name: Tom
search_queries.priority_reason: Lowest pipeline value (£100-150) but genuine intent shown (pricing question, acknowledged quote-form pain unprompted). LinkedIn is last channel before archiving.
search_queries.linkedin_sector_priority: low — trades, first name only. Attempt because lead showed genuine intent.
search_queries.fallback_query_1: site:linkedin.com/in carpentry joinery "Ripon" "North Yorkshire" owner director
search_queries.profile_verification_required: Confirm profile is clearly the owner/operator of Ripon Road Carpentry in Ripon. Do NOT connect to a generic Tom in carpentry elsewhere in Yorkshire.
search_queries.do_not_send_if: IMAP reveals any email reply since 2026-04-04.
search_queries.note_type_on_find: post_reference if post found within last 60 days — write bespoke note. If no recent post: use generic note.
search_queries.seasonal_context: May is start of peak carpentry season. Tom may have been too busy to reply in April. Pain point (no quote form) is structural and ongoing.

hands_today_monday_2026_05_04_session_pl.execution_target: 2 hours maximum for entire LinkedIn session. All searches, profile checks, post scans, and connection requests in a single sitting.
priority_order: 1. IMAP SWEEP for leads 3, 22, 1 — mandatory first (before 09:00). Any reply cancels that lead's LinkedIn note.
2. Full IMAP sweep of all cohort leads — 09:00-09:30.
3. Google search for each eligible lead in priority order: Claire then Rob then Tom.
4. Open profile, verify ownership, scroll posts from last 60 days.
5. If post found: write bespoke post_reference note under 200 chars. If no post: use pre-generated generic note from linkedin_connection_notes.json.
7. Write linkedin_profiles.json IMMEDIATELY after session — every lead searched, even if no profile found or note not sent.
hands_today_monday_2026_05_04_session_pl.post_reference_protocol: For each lead: (1) run Google query, (2) open profile, (3) scroll posts from last 60 days, (4) if post found: write bespoke note under 200 chars, (5) if no post: use pre-generated generic note. Count every character including spaces.
hands_today_monday_2026_05_04_session_pl.char_counting_reminder: 200 chars is a hard limit. Test in notepad before sending.
hands_today_monday_2026_05_04_session_pl.failure_protocol: If HANDS cannot execute today Monday 2026-05-04: do NOT execute Tuesday 2026-05-05 (bank holiday). Tuesday 2026-05-06 is the absolute last acceptable day. After Tuesday: warm pipeline connection opportunity is stale — archive leads 1/3/22. Log reason in CHANGELOG.md immediately.
